question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has already been released on April 10, 2026. According to the official BLS data and multiple news sources, the CPI increased by only 0.3% in March 2026 on a seasonally adjusted basis, which is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for a YES resolution. Since the actual reported figure of 0.3% is definitively below 0.9%, the market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty.
key_factors: <cite index="2-1,14-1,14-7">Official BLS report confirms March 2026 CPI increased 0.3% monthly</cite>
<cite index="1-1,1-10">CPI data was released April 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET as scheduled</cite>
Market requires increase greater than 0.9% for YES resolution
0.3% actual increase is well below the 0.9% threshold
risks: Potential data revision by BLS (though highly unlikely to change from 0.3% to >0.9%)
